description_long: This object consists of three vertically oriented, irregularly shaped stones with rough, angular surfaces and a natural, weathered appearance. The stones are closely grouped, each featuring distinct ridges, crevices, and subtle color variations in shades of gray and brown. Their surfaces are matte and uneven, with visible textural details that suggest exposure to the elements over time. The tallest stone stands prominently at the center, flanked by two slightly shorter stones, creating a visually dynamic cluster. There are no artificial markings or carvings, and the stones appear solid and heavy, typical of natural rock formations. The overall form suggests use as a physical barrier or decorative feature in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A cluster of three tall, rough, naturally weathered stones with angular surfaces and subtle gray-brown color variations, arranged closely together. The stones have a rugged, matte texture and appear solid and heavy.
description_view_0: The stones are seen from an angle that highlights their verticality and the close grouping, with the tallest stone in the center and the others partially visible behind.
description_view_1: This view shows the stones from the front, revealing all three stones side by side, each with distinct surface textures and shapes.
description_view_2: The stones are viewed from the side, emphasizing their narrow profiles and the depth of the central stone.
description_view_3: A rear view of the stones, showing the backs of all three and their angular, uneven surfaces.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped as a decorative barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular, natural mottling and subtle layering with no artificial carvings; surface features are random and organic.
attribute_weathering: Rough and matte with visible signs of natural weathering, such as small pits and crevices, but no significant erosion or lichen.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with landscaped elements, often surrounded by grass, gravel, or low vegetation, and may contrast with nearby paved surfaces.
